assets.name: lecture_06_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 02 gives the best balance of completeness and legibility. It preserves the full board layout for this moment: the separate state spaces at the top, the tensor-product relation, the basis-state mapping into a composite ket, and the beginning of the orthonormality relation below. Candidate 03 has slightly less occlusion in the middle line, but it crops more of the top-board structure; Candidate 01 is more blocked by the lecturer; Candidate 04 is too cropped to the right to serve as the main witness.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: the general vector in the tensor product is

assets.name: lecture_06_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 02 captures the strongest mathematical payoff of the moment: the bracketed product-state form at the top and the fully written expansion into the composite basis below. It is more useful for note writing than Candidate 01, which is more occluded by the lecturer, and more complete than Candidates 03 and 04, which crop away too much of the second factor on the right.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: It's a vector drawn in the composite system as a tensor product
assets.caption_hint: Product state expanded in the composite basis

assets.name: lecture_06_figure_04.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 03 is the best compromise between completeness and legibility. It keeps the first relation fully readable, \sigma_x acting on |u i> to give |d i>, while also showing the second line far enough along to make clear that the analogous action on |d i> is being written. Candidate 04 is slightly later but crops too much of the already completed top equation; Candidates 01 and 02 are earlier and leave the second line too unfinished.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: sigma x on down i equals up i.
assets.caption_hint: Action of \sigma_x on composite basis states

assets.name: lecture_06_figure_05.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 04 is the most settled and least occluded frame in this subtitle window. It keeps the full set of operator-action rules visible on the board, with the lecturer no longer blocking the right-hand sides. Earlier candidates either obscure part of the equations or catch the board in a less finished state.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: Those equations sigma x acting on u
assets.caption_hint: Pauli action on composite basis kets

assets.name: lecture_06_figure_06.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 04 is the most finished frame in this window. It preserves the earlier basis-state action on the left while also showing the new bracketed commutator expression furthest along on the right. The earlier candidates catch the commutator being started and are less useful as a settled witness for note writing.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: Commutator of sigma x with tau x equals zero.
